task.name: nhs-hours-engine-fix
task.working_directory: /home/filipejunqueira/code/nhs-hour-log — run `pwd` to confirm. Do not use ~ in paths: inside this container HOME is /home/filipejunqueira/containers/claude-home. Use relative or absolute paths.
task.context:
A logic audit was completed 2026-07-06 and its report is at logic-audit_2026-07-06.md (untracked, 238 lines, findings F1-F6). Its verdict: the hours arithmetic is CORRECT and nothing overstates worked hours. It applied no fixes because engine_v2/** was locked, so each finding carries a patch for deliberate application. A companion characterisation suite lives at audit/test_characterization.py (29 checks, passing as of 07-06).
This run APPLIES those patches. The engine_v2/** deny rule has been lifted for this run only — that is a deliberate, temporary exception, not a new default.

authority.rule: This task specification OVERRIDES CLAUDE.md wherever they conflict.
authority.override: CLAUDE.md line 16 states "Do NOT modify engine_v2/ — complete, audited, locked (also denied in .claude/settings.json)". That instruction is SUSPENDED for this run only. The corresponding deny rules have been removed from .claude/settings.json for the same reason. Modifying engine_v2/ is the explicit purpose of this task — do not stall, do not ask whether you are allowed, and do not treat the CLAUDE.md line as a blocker.
authority.scope_of_suspension: The suspension covers ONLY the F1-F6 patches and the test repointing described in steps 2-4. It is not licence for unrelated refactoring, tidying, renaming, reformatting or "improvements" anywhere under engine_v2/. Every byte you change there must trace to a named finding.
authority.restoration: The deny rules and the CLAUDE.md instruction are the correct default and will be restored after this run. Mention this in your handover report so it is not forgotten.

task.critical_safety_rule: This engine feeds a payroll submission. If ANY change you make alters a computed figure for the current dataset — total minutes, band split, class split, per-day values — STOP IMMEDIATELY, do not proceed, and report exactly which figure moved and why. Robustness and validation fixes must be output-neutral for valid input. F6 (the overnight split dropping one minute) is DOCUMENTED behaviour: do not "fix" it without asking.
task.characterisation_suite_semantics: audit/test_characterization.py pins CURRENT behaviour, including the defects, so that fixing a defect shows up as a visible test failure. That is by design. When a pinned-defect test fails after you apply its patch, that is the guardrail working: update that test deliberately, state in your report which test you changed and why. NEVER edit a test merely to make the suite green.
task.step: Before changing anything, establish and report: - Line count, date range and first/last row of engine_v2/data/filipe_working_hours_log.csv - Whether it is byte-identical to either data/exports/hours_export_2026-06-25.csv or data/exports/hours_export_2026-07-18.csv (use cmp) - Full diff between the two staged exports — how many rows changed, and the total-minutes delta - Result of running audit/test_characterization.py as-is - Result of running the engine's own suite: python3 tests/test_*.py from engine_v2/ - Whether engine_v2/web_data.json is stale relative to the current CSV STOP after reporting. Do not proceed to step 1 until I confirm which dataset is authoritative.

Once I have named the authoritative export: - Copy it to engine_v2/data/filipe_working_hours_log.csv (canonical, normalised). - Copy it also to tests/fixtures/hours_<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv where the date is the LATEST work date present in the data — derive it, do not use today's date. - Leave data/exports/ untouched: those are raw provenance and accumulate. - Note the header difference between exports (column 6 is "Main accomplishments in the day" in one and " Main events in the day" — with a leading space — in the other). Confirm the parser handles both or is insensitive to it; if not, that is a new finding. - Note that time formats are inconsistent WITHIN a single file (8:45 unpadded alongside 08:03 padded). Confirm the parser handles both; if not, new finding.

F1 (HIGH) — the regression suite is broken: engine_v2/tests/test_core.py:22 and test_emit.py:18 hardcode data/filipe_working_hours_log_25-06-2026.csv, which does not exist, and their hand-derived totals are stale. Fix: repoint them at the FROZEN FIXTURE from step 1, never at the live CSV, and re-derive their expected values from that fixture. Show the old and new expected values side by side in your report.

Apply the patches carried in logic-audit_2026-07-06.md for F2 (Minutes = inf crashes ingest with an uncaught OverflowError), F3 (invariant net misses duplicates fitting within the day span) and F4 (Minutes cross-check has silent paths). Apply them ONE AT A TIME, running both suites after each, and report each result before moving to the next. Any output change for valid data triggers the critical safety rule.
F5 and F6 are documentation divergences. Update the documentation to match actual behaviour — do NOT change the behaviour. State clearly which document you edited.

Regenerate engine_v2/web_data.json from the canonical CSV via scripts/regen.sh. Report the before/after figures explicitly: total minutes, distinct days, band split, class split, schema version. The previously recorded 22 days / 12 619 min / bands 5400-3600-3619 is STALE REFERENCE — state whether your figures match or differ, and by how much.

- logic-audit_2026-07-06.md links audit/test_characterisation.py (British -isation) but the file is audit/test_characterization.py (-ization). Fix the link; do not rename the file unless renaming is trivially safe and nothing else references it. - docs/CONTINUATION.md shows as deleted but unstaged. Report what it was and whether the deletion is intentional. Do not resolve it yourself. - audit/session_plan_reference_only_not_commit.md is named as excluded from commits — respect that. - .claude/settings.json is MODIFIED (deny rules emptied) and .claude/settings.json.bak-20260718-190154 is untracked scratch. Neither belongs in a commit — exclude both from your proposed grouping.

Run both suites one final time and paste the output. Report git status. Propose a commit grouping (which files in which commit, with messages) but DO NOT COMMIT. Remind me to restore the engine_v2/** deny rules from .claude/settings.json.bak-20260718-190154.

out_of_scope.item: No website or Vue work — that is the next task, separately prompted.

invariants.invariant: No computed payroll figure changes without an explicit stop-and-ask.
No test reads a live data path at runtime; all pin to tests/fixtures/.
No test is edited solely to make the suite pass.
Every byte changed under engine_v2/ traces to a named finding F1-F6.
